results.editorial:
Tai Kuk Wah Si Buddhist Society operates a small residential care home attached to its temple complex in Taman Pertama, Cheras — a neighbourhood that straddles the Selangor-KL border along the Cheras corridor. The society is primarily known as a Buddhist religious organisation, and its elder care arm appears to be a welfare extension of that mission rather than a commercial nursing facility. With 20 listed beds and a phone number as the only public contact, the home is modest in scale and appears to cater to elderly residents who are Buddhist or who have no other means of support.

Public information on this home is sparse. Google Maps reviewers (29 reviews, 4.6 stars) suggest the home is operational and well-regarded within its community, but no website, Facebook page, or regulatory filing was found in open sources. The care model appears to rely on volunteer support and in-kind donations consistent with Buddhist welfare home traditions in the Klang Valley — a pattern shared by similar society-run homes that use temple networks for volunteer doctor visits and general upkeep.

Families considering this home should visit in person before committing. Confirm whether it is registered with the Jabatan Kebajikan Masyarakat (JKM), what medical oversight is in place, and whether professional nursing staff or volunteers provide daily care. The home's Buddhist religious character is likely embedded in daily routines, so families of other faiths should establish comfort levels upfront. Verify visiting policies, food arrangements, and the practical pathway for hospital transfers, as these details are not publicly documented.

facts.red_flags: No website, no Facebook, very limited public information. JKM registration status unknown. Staffing model unclear — may rely heavily on volunteers.

results.editorial:
Persatuan Kebajikan Dan Social Kim Loo Ting — known in Chinese as 甘露亭 — is a Buddhist welfare association operating a residential home for elderly homeless individuals in the Taman Ayer Panas (Setapak) area of the Kuala Lumpur-Selangor fringe. The home follows a charity model: it primarily accepts elderly residents who have been discharged from government hospitals with no family support and no fixed address, making it distinct from conventional fee-paying nursing homes. Capacity is listed at 25 beds, with men and women housed in separate rooms within the same premises.

Volunteer doctors from Tung Shin Hospital conduct free medical consultations for residents every Sunday, providing basic clinical oversight. The home maintains high cleanliness standards, with able-bodied residents contributing to daily upkeep alongside a small number of paid helpers. The Facebook page (listed as the website in the data) reflects the organisation's charitable outreach. The facility has operated for many years and is affiliated with Buddhist welfare networks in the Klang Valley, lending it some community credibility despite its modest infrastructure.

This home serves a specific social welfare function and is not suited to families seeking fee-based private nursing care. If you are enquiring on behalf of a destitute elderly person, call the centre directly at 03-40232350 to discuss admission criteria. Confirm the current JKM registration status, the frequency and scope of the volunteer doctor programme, and whether any nursing-level care (not just caregiver support) is available on-site. Families of paying residents should clarify what ongoing medical management looks like for complex conditions.

facts.red_flags: Charity welfare model — not a conventional nursing home. Admission criteria primarily for homeless elderly. Medical coverage is volunteer-based (weekly only). Limited paid staff.

results.editorial:
Woodrose Senior Residences 2 is the male wing of Woodrose Healthcare's Seksyen 11 campus in Shah Alam, Selangor — a pair of adjacent facilities that together form what is likely the most established Shariah-compliant nursing home operation in the Klang Valley. Woodrose Senior Residences Sdn Bhd describes itself as "the most well established nursing home in Selangor" and emerged from a merger between Zara Aisyah Care Centre and MLL Sdn Bhd. Residence 2, on Jalan Gamelan, is specifically designated for male residents and operates under Shariah oversight developed with an in-house Ustaz who shaped the facility's SOPs for food, personal care, daily activities, and spiritual guidance.

The Shariah compliance framework at Woodrose is designed to address the genuine clinical and religious tension of having non-mahram caregivers attend to opposite-gender residents — a significant concern for observant Muslim families. By separating the homes by gender, Woodrose allows for same-gender caregiving, halal-certified food, Islamic daily routines, and religious programming including observances during Ramadan. Services across the Woodrose network include dementia care, Alzheimer's care, stroke rehabilitation, palliative care, physiotherapy, day care, and short-term respite. The campus features indoor and outdoor activity spaces, and rooms at Residence 2 are described as spacious with good access facilities.

Woodrose is not the cheapest option in Shah Alam and is clearly positioned at the mid-to-upper end of the market. Families should ask about specific capacity at Residence 2 versus Residence 1, the current Ustaz oversight structure, waiting times for admission, and pricing tiers by dependency level. It is worth confirming whether the facility holds a valid JKM licence and whether there is an on-call or visiting physician arrangement — details not published on the website.

facts.care_types: Nursing home, Dementia care, Palliative care, Respite, Day care, Rehabilitation
facts.red_flags: Pricing and capacity not published. JKM licence number not publicly listed. Confirm same-gender caregiver policy is consistently implemented.

results.editorial:
Woodrose Senior Residences 1 is the female wing of the Woodrose Healthcare campus in Seksyen 11, Shah Alam — located on Jalan Silat, a short distance from the male Residence 2 on Jalan Gamelan. Operated by Woodrose Senior Residences Sdn Bhd, this is the earlier of the two Seksyen 11 homes and features 2-bedded and 4-bedded rooms along with a swimming pool, which can be used for hydrotherapy and light aquatic exercise. The facility caters to Muslim female elderly residents who require nursing home, dementia, palliative, respite, or day care services in a Shariah-compliant environment.

The Shariah compliance programme at Woodrose — shared across both Seksyen 11 residences — was developed with an in-house Ustaz to govern caregiving SOPs, dietary preparation (halal food), spiritual programming, and the management of personal care in a gender-appropriate manner. Female residents benefit from female-only caregiving staff, Islamic daily routines, and a care environment designed with their religious and emotional comfort in mind. The broader Woodrose service offer includes dementia and Alzheimer's management, stroke rehabilitation, physiotherapy, and palliative care, all available to residents of Residence 1. The Google rating for Residence 1 (3.9 stars, 26 reviews) is notably lower than Residence 2 (4.2 stars), which is worth exploring further with the operator.

Pricing is not published and should be obtained directly by calling the shared line (+60 12-709 8102). Ask specifically about current capacity at Residence 1, staff-to-resident ratios, and how high-dependency or bed-bound residents are managed. The lower Google rating relative to sibling homes warrants a candid conversation and, ideally, an in-person visit before placement. Confirm JKM registration status and the frequency of physician oversight.

facts.care_types: Nursing home, Dementia care, Palliative care, Respite, Day care, Rehabilitation
facts.red_flags: Lower Google rating (3.9) than sibling homes — worth investigating. Pricing and capacity unpublished. JKM licence not publicly listed.

results.editorial:
Cality Care Malaysia is a home health care agency and staffing service headquartered in the KL Traders Square building along Jalan Gombak, with its Google Maps presence tagged to the Gombak area of Selangor. The company is the Malaysian arm of ARC Group Australia and positions itself around delivering Australian-standard home care — a useful framing that signals structured care protocols, documented care plans, and trained rather than informally recruited staff. The care team includes Registered Nurses, Medical Assistants, Physiotherapists, and Allied Health professionals, as well as in-house trained caregivers certified through their own training programme.

Cality's service model is home-based rather than residential: they dispatch carers to clients' own homes rather than operating a nursing facility with beds. Services cover personal hygiene, dressing, meal support, rehabilitation exercises, companionship, medication management, wound care, and appointment accompaniment. A minimum daily engagement of six hours is required, with flexible arrangements and no exit fees — a client-friendly policy that distinguishes them from agencies with lock-in contracts. Google rates this provider at 5 stars across 25 reviews, which is consistent with the profile of a boutique agency where the team knows each client personally.

Cality is appropriate for families who want a professionally managed home care team rather than a nursing home placement, particularly for post-hospitalisation recovery, chronic condition management at home, or supplementing family care for a dependent elder. Call them during office hours (Monday to Friday, 9am–5pm) at 011-6226 3227 to discuss the specific care needs, confirm geographic coverage for your area of Selangor or KL, and obtain a detailed quote. Ask about minimum engagement hours for lower-dependency cases and whether overnight or 24-hour live-in arrangements are available.

facts.care_types: Home health care, Nursing agency, Physiotherapy, Allied health
facts.red_flags: Home-based service only — not a residential facility. Pricing not published. Office hours limited to weekdays.

results.editorial:
Sunstar Golden Care Centre is listed as a nursing home in Bandar Sunway, one of Selangor's most densely served elder care corridors given its proximity to Sunway Medical Centre and a large residential population. The facility holds a 5-star Google rating across 24 reviews — a strong signal of resident and family satisfaction for a centre of this size — but has no published website, Facebook page, or other online presence beyond its Google Maps listing, making independent verification of services difficult.

Bandar Sunway's care landscape is competitive: families in this corridor can choose from Sunway Sanctuary (the premium Sunway Group retirement living product) down to smaller community homes. Sunstar's positioning within this market is unclear from available information — it is not featured in aggregator roundups of the top Subang Jaya nursing homes, which may reflect its smaller scale or newer establishment. The phone number (+60 14-660 1127) and its Google presence confirm it is operational, and the rating suggests a positively regarded environment.

Given the absence of public information, an in-person visit is the only reliable way to assess Sunstar Golden Care Centre. When visiting, ask about JKM registration and licence status, the range of dependency levels accepted (low, medium, high, bed-bound), nurse-to-resident ratios, visiting doctor arrangements, and current pricing. Confirm whether the 5-star rating reflects a stable and established operation or a relatively small review pool from early months of operation.

facts.red_flags: No website or social media presence. Not featured in known aggregator lists. JKM registration unconfirmed. Minimal public information — visit required.

results.editorial:
Golden Years Senior Residence in Kota Kemuning is a medically oriented nursing home set within a quiet residential pocket of Shah Alam at 4, Jalan Anggerik Aranda 31/170F — a biophilic environment with gardens designed to reduce the institutional feel that families often dread. The facility was founded by healthcare professionals: the clinical leadership team includes a GP, an Internal Medicine Specialist with over 15 years of experience, a Registered Nurse Matron with 35-plus years in clinical care, and an in-house physiotherapist. A second location in Bandar Kinrara, Puchong, and a home nursing service covering surrounding areas extend the group's reach.

Services at Kota Kemuning are structured around four care modes: long-term residential care for chronic and progressive conditions, short-term post-hospital recovery, adult day care (8:30am–6pm weekdays, RM2,000–2,300/month), and home nursing visits within the local area at RM30/hour. Monthly residential fees are tiered by dependency: RM2,950 for low, RM3,450 for medium, and RM3,950 for high, with short-term stays priced at RM220–300/day. Occupational therapy is available at RM200/session, physiotherapy at RM100/session for non-package residents. A telemedical doctor standby service is listed for acute medical needs, and residents receive daily vital sign monitoring and annual health screenings.

A RM3,500 refundable deposit is required on admission for residential care (RM2,000 for day care). Families should ask about current bed availability — the Kota Kemuning facility appears to be a multi-unit operation — and clarify what "standby telemedical doctor" means in practice versus an on-site physician. Confirm whether the Internal Medicine Specialist provides regular in-person consultations and at what frequency. The Google rating (4.7 stars, 24 reviews) and the published pricing transparency make this one of the more accountable small operators in Shah Alam.

facts.care_types: Nursing home, Adult day care, Short-term care, Home nursing
facts.red_flags: Telemedical doctor coverage — clarify how acute on-site emergencies are handled. Confirm whether Internal Medicine Specialist visits are routine or on-call only.

results.editorial:
Attia Global Care Centre Subang Jaya is part of the Attia Nursing Care network, which has grown since 2011 from a single nursing home into six branches across the Klang Valley and Ipoh. The Subang Jaya branch sits at No. 7, Jalan SS 19/2 in the established SS19 neighbourhood, providing long-term assisted living care in a facility described as homely rather than clinical in character — air-conditioned rooms with attached bathrooms, a large living area, elevators for accessibility, and nurse call systems. Weekly doctor visits, hospital transfer support, and daily monitoring of blood pressure, glucose, and diabetes parameters are part of the standard offering.

The medical services at this branch are notably broad for a community nursing home: medication management, speech therapy, physiotherapy, acupuncture, wound dressing, tracheostomy management, and two-hourly repositioning to prevent pressure sores. Day care is available alongside full residential care. Recreational outings and an in-house activity programme are listed as resident engagement features. The network's longevity and multi-branch presence suggest stable operational management and the economies of scale needed to maintain consistent staffing. The SS19 location is contactable at 03-5611 0548 or WhatsApp 016-647 1674.

Visits to the centre are by appointment only — call ahead to arrange. Families should ask specifically about current capacity at this branch (not network-wide), the on-site nurse staffing ratio, whether the weekly doctor is a GP or specialist, and how complex dementia or palliative cases are managed. Attia's multi-site model means quality can vary between branches, so an in-person assessment of the SS19 premises is worthwhile before commitment. Pricing is not published; request a personalised quote based on dependency level.

facts.care_types: Nursing home, Day care, Assisted living
facts.red_flags: Pricing not published. Visits by appointment only. Multi-branch operator — quality may vary; SS19-specific staff ratios should be verified.

results.editorial:
EHA Bayview Eldercare is located at 11, Jalan Budiman, Budiman Business Park, Bandar Sungai Long — a light-industrial enclave on the Cheras-Kajang fringe that puts it within reach of residents from Sungai Long, Cheras, and Kajang. The centre is part of EHA ElderCare, which was established in 2009 (formerly Justlife ElderCare Home) and now operates eight major facilities across Johor and the Klang Valley, making it one of Malaysia's more experienced multi-site eldercare operators. Its Google listing uses the Chinese name 温馨疗养别墅 (Wēnxīn Liáoyǎng Biéshù — roughly "warm healing villa"), which signals a Chinese-community orientation alongside its English marketing.

EHA's care model is built on what the group calls "Function Focused Care" — tailoring daily routines to maximise each resident's remaining physical and functional capability rather than defaulting to full dependency. The centre accepts able-bodied, semi-able-bodied, and bed-bound residents, and offers three service modes: long-term assisted living, short-term respite (1–6 months), and day care. Across the KL and Selangor network, long-term rates are listed from RM3,200/month and day care from RM1,500/month, with short-term respite from RM120/day. Shared and private rooms are available; meals are personalised with chef-prepared food. Staff have 15-plus years of cumulative group experience with certified nurses and trained caregivers.

Contact the Sungai Long branch directly at +60 17-755 6997 or ask.ehagroup@gmail.com to confirm current pricing at this specific location — rates may differ from the network-wide figures. Ask about the nurse-to-resident ratio, how tracheostomy or PEG-tube residents are managed, and whether a visiting doctor arrangement is in place. The Budiman Business Park address is functional rather than residential, so families visiting for the first time should confirm the exact unit and parking access.

facts.care_types: Nursing home, Assisted living, Adult day care, Respite
facts.red_flags: Industrial park address — verify unit and accessibility. Pricing confirmed for network but not Sungai Long specifically. Confirm on-site nurse staffing and visiting physician frequency.

results.editorial:
Genesis Life Care Centre PJ is the flagship location of a five-branch Selangor-and-Johor nursing home group, occupying a purpose-built space at Lot 1, Petaling Utama Avenue, Jalan PJS 1/50 in Taman Petaling Utama, Petaling Jaya. Established in 2020, the PJ centre is the group's most clinically well-resourced site — with 50-plus beds, a dedicated memory care wing, and a rehabilitation suite staffed by senior physiotherapists and occupational therapists. It holds Ministry of Health approval and a 4.9-star Google rating across 58 reviews, the strongest rating in the Genesis network.

The clinical offer here is broader than most community nursing homes. Dementia and Alzheimer's residents are placed in the dedicated memory care wing, overseen by psychologists who run structured cognitive stimulation programmes tailored to individual residents. Stroke rehabilitation, post-operative recovery, and palliative care are all offered as distinct care pathways rather than being bundled under generic "nursing home" care. The 30-plus person care team includes specialist therapists, nursing supervisors, and 24/7 nursing availability. Pricing starts from RM2,500/month — competitive for PJ — with costs scaling by dependency level and room type. Visiting hours are 9am–6pm daily.

Genesis Life Care PJ is one of the more transparent operators in Selangor: the website publishes care pathway descriptions, team credentials, location details, and a cost calculator. Families should use the calculator as a starting point, then call +60 19-295 0457 to confirm current bed availability in the specific ward needed (memory care vs general nursing vs rehab). Ask about the staffing ratio overnight versus daytime, the psychological programme intensity for dementia residents, and what the discharge pathway looks like for post-op recovery patients who stabilise. The 2020 establishment date means the centre is relatively new — confirm how the team handles first-ever complex care scenarios.

facts.care_types: Nursing home, Dementia care, Stroke rehabilitation, Palliative care, Post-operative care, Respite
facts.red_flags: Established 2020 — relatively young operation. Confirm overnight nurse ratios and how complex or unstable medical cases are managed. Memory care wing capacity not specified.
